Quality Education.reason: The course provides "Professional Competence" and skills in risk management and "career planning," contributing to skills for employment (Target 4.4).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The principles of "risk management" and "insurance" are foundational for increasing access to financial services for enterprises and building resilient infrastructure (Targets 9.1, 9.3).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: By teaching about "insurance" as a form of social protection (Target 10.4), the course addresses mechanisms that can promote economic inclusion and reduce inequality (Target 10.2).

Climate Action.reason: The principles of "risk management" are essential for strengthening resilience to climate-related hazards and natural disasters (Target 13.1), though not explicitly covered.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course analyzes "insurance disputes" through "litigation cases," directly engaging with the rule of law and access to justice for all (Target 16.3).
